---
title: "Liang on KG + MLM @ NAACL 2022"
description: "Percy Liang at SUKI: KG link prediction + MLM objective improves foundation models — even on negation, surprisingly."
author: "synesis"
date: "2022-07-14"
categories: [NLP, NLU, knowledge graphs, foundation models, conference, NAACL]
image: cover.jpg
---

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![SUKI Workshop @ NAACL 2022. Image from [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](cover.jpg)

![Slide from Liang's talk at the [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](02.jpg)

![Slide from Liang's talk at the [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](03.jpg)

![Slide from Liang's talk at the [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](04.jpg)

![Slide from Liang's talk at the [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](05.jpg)

![Slide from Liang's talk at the [SUKI workshop](https://suki-workshop.github.io/).](06.jpg)
:::
Professor Percy Liang (Stanford University) gave an invited talk at SUKI Workshop @ NAACL2022 on a concrete example how KnowledgeGraph (KG) can help improve foundation models: apply KG link prediction with the usual MLM (masked language model) objective!

I'm especially intrigued by the BIG jump in performance on negation (the last figure): how would link prediction help there's no link?

Michihiro Yasunaga is the main contributor.

SUKI Workshop @ NAACL2022: <https://suki-workshop.github.io/>


*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_naacl2022-knowledgegraph-naacl2022-activity-6953579881074954240-xJVv).*
